title: WTF to do pre-AGI off-hours to ensure legacy is right
html:
WTF to do pre-AGI off-hours to ensure legacy is right

Source: 2025-01-06 Apple Note. Two-line note. Title carries all the weight; body is essentially "build apps." A glimpse of how Yahir frames his off-hours work: as a finite pre-AGI window in which legacy-relevant moves still matter.

TL;DR

  • The framing: there is a pre-AGI window and what Yahir does inside it shapes whether his "legacy is right."
  • The action item: "build apps." That's the entire body.
  • Sits alongside 2025 01 01 project 2025 (5 days earlier) — same week's intent-setting; this note narrows "build passion products" into "build apps, before AGI changes the rules."
  • The same urgency thread runs through later 2025 corpus: 2025 08 29 irl lore prd, the Brotein launch, the eventual Rankett bet.
  • Quietly reframes "legacy" as builder-output rather than financial inheritance — distinct from the mom house by the river goal thread.

Pattern observed

Yahir uses AGI as a deadline metaphor here, not a futurology pose. The note doesn't theorize about AGI; it just treats its arrival as the cutoff and asks "given that, what do I do off-hours?" Same pragmatic register as the Karpathy notes he was reading around this period.

The sparseness is the signal. Two lines, one of them blank. Yahir often writes himself a single-line directive (compare 2026 01 22 1212026 "I am a fucking killer", "Are you ferociously obsessed?") — the title-as-thesis pattern.

Open questions

  • What does "legacy is right" mean in his frame at this moment — financial security for family, public-facing builds, or skill-accumulation?
  • The note bridges to and brotein later in the year — which app(s) was he visualizing here?
